title: periploca laevigata subsp. angustifolia
intro: Periploca laevigata subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved blue passion flower vine, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular disease due to its high concentration of alkaloids. The health benefits of Periploca laevigata subsp. angustifolia include reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can help to protect against various diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
